cell_id: 154.sample_comment,"Rhodophycota indet.(non-calc.crusts) has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Hard slate with igneous intrusions forming high (to 5m) ridges, with many overhangs and vertical faces. Exposed seaward sides had much Lithophyllum / Lithothamnion, Corallina spp, Barnacles, Actina equina and Nucella lapilus. On the sheltered sides there were many sponges (Halichondria, Hymeniacidon, Grantia) as well as Polysiphonia spp. and other filamentous reds. A large plateau extended beyond the ridges to LWST. Little rock was visible, being covered in encrusting red algae and Mastocarpus stellatus and kelp. The pools contained few boulders but there was an increased diversity of algae and fauna.","Fucaceae, Nucella, Patella, Trochidae has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Chthamalus montagui has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. The shore consisted of a fairly level, mica shist which contained many fissures and gullies, backed by mud cliffs. Rockpools had been formed in depressions on the surface. The area is characteristic for barnacle cover and encrusting red algae on drying and non-drying surfaces. Seaweed cover was poor
new_value: except at the lower shore where encrusting reds were dominant. The shore appeared to be quite exposed from all southerly directions (SW - ESE). This may make it unsuitable for crabs and other large mobile spp. Cthamalus montagui and Corallina in the pools were characteristic as were Patella
